questions.q: On whose behalf did Krishna travel to Hastinapura as the final peace envoy?
questions.explanation: Krishna went as the last ambassador of the Pandavas to seek a peaceful settlement before the war.

questions.q: What was the minimal demand Krishna placed before the Kuru court?
options: Five villages for the five brothers
questions.explanation: Krishna asked that the Pandavas be given just five villages, one for each brother, to avert the war.

questions.q: Who declared that the Pandavas would not get even land enough for a needle's point?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana refused every compromise, vowing not to yield without war even as much land as a needle's point could pierce.

questions.q: What did Duryodhana plot to do to Krishna during the peace embassy?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana conspired to capture the ambassador, a plan that collapsed when Krishna revealed his divine form.

questions.q: What did Krishna reveal in the court when the plot against him was attempted?
options: The future of the Kuru line
questions.explanation: Krishna displayed his blazing Vishvarupa, the universal form containing gods and worlds, before the stunned assembly.

questions.q: At whose home did Krishna choose to dine while in Hastinapura?
questions.explanation: Krishna took his meal at the humble home of Vidura, whose affection he valued above royal feasts.

questions.q: To which city did Krishna travel for the peace embassy?
questions.explanation: Krishna journeyed to Hastinapura, the Kuru capital, to negotiate directly with Dhritarashtra and Duryodhana.

questions.q: What became certain once Krishna's embassy failed?
questions.explanation: With the last attempt at peace refused, both sides prepared for the great war at Kurukshetra.

questions.q: In which book of the Mahabharata does the peace embassy take place?
questions.explanation: Krishna's mission to Hastinapura is narrated in the Udyoga Parva, the book of preparations for war.

questions.q: Whose grand hospitality did Krishna pointedly decline in Hastinapura?
questions.explanation: Krishna refused Duryodhana's lavish feast and lodging, choosing instead the simple home of Vidura.

questions.q: Why did Krishna refuse to eat at Duryodhana's feast?
options: He had vowed to fast until peace was made
Food offered with enmity should not be eaten
The elders had warned him of poison
An envoy may never eat in a rival court
questions.explanation: Krishna said one eats another's food only when offered with love or in dire need, and Duryodhana's table offered neither.

questions.q: Who was granted divine sight to behold Krishna's universal form in the Kuru court?
questions.explanation: The blind king Dhritarashtra begged for vision, and Krishna granted him momentary divine sight to witness the Vishvarupa.

questions.q: Whom did Krishna take aside on his chariot while leaving Hastinapura?
questions.explanation: Departing the city, Krishna invited Karna onto his chariot for a private conversation that revealed a great secret.

questions.q: What secret did Krishna disclose to Karna during that chariot ride?
options: The plan of the war formations
The day fixed for the first battle
That Karna was Kunti's firstborn son
That Bhishma would not fight Shikhandi
questions.explanation: Krishna told Karna he was born to Kunti before her marriage, making him the eldest brother of the Pandavas.

questions.q: What did Krishna offer Karna to draw him away from Duryodhana's side?
options: The kingship as the eldest Pandava
questions.explanation: Krishna promised that as the eldest son of Kunti, Karna would be crowned king and served by the Pandavas themselves.

questions.q: How did Karna answer Krishna's offer of kingship?
options: He asked for time to decide
He demanded proof of his birth
questions.explanation: Karna refused to abandon Duryodhana, who had given him honor and friendship when the world mocked him.

questions.q: Whose fiery message urging the Pandavas to fight did Krishna carry back from Hastinapura?
questions.explanation: Kunti sent through Krishna the stirring tale of Vidula, a mother's command that her sons fight like kshatriyas.

questions.q: Which group of elders pressed Dhritarashtra to accept Krishna's peace terms?
questions.explanation: Bhishma, Drona, and Vidura all urged the king to restrain Duryodhana and give the Pandavas their due.

questions.q: Before Krishna's mission, who had come to the Pandavas as Dhritarashtra's own envoy?
questions.explanation: Dhritarashtra had earlier sent his charioteer Sanjaya to the Pandava camp with vague pleas for peace without restitution.

questions.q: How did Duryodhana respond when the elders rebuked him before Krishna?
options: He begged his father to decide
He challenged Krishna to single combat
He stormed out of the assembly
questions.explanation: Unable to bear the rebukes, Duryodhana angrily walked out of the court with his counselors.

questions.q: Why did Krishna undertake the embassy even though he expected it to fail?
options: So no blame for the war would rest on the Pandavas
To count the strength of the Kaurava host
To win over Bhishma and Drona
To delay the war until spring
questions.explanation: Krishna said the mission would free the Pandavas of blame and let the world see that every path to peace had been tried.

questions.q: How did most of the court react to the blaze of Krishna's revealed universal form?
options: They fled the hall in terror
They fell into a deathlike sleep
They shut their eyes, unable to bear the light
They saw nothing unusual at all
questions.explanation: The kings closed or averted their eyes before the unbearable radiance, while the assembled sages looked on in awe.

questions.q: What is the name of the sub-parva that narrates Krishna's embassy to Hastinapura?
questions.explanation: The mission is told in the Bhagavad-yana Parva, the section of the Udyoga Parva covering the Lord's journey as envoy.

questions.q: Which town, infamous as the site of the house of lac, was among the five villages the Pandavas requested?
questions.explanation: Varanavata, where the lac-house plot nearly burned the Pandavas alive, was pointedly among the villages they asked for.

questions.q: Which Yadava warrior accompanied Krishna on the journey to Hastinapura?
questions.explanation: Krishna took the warrior Satyaki with him on the chariot when he set out for the Kuru court.

questions.q: Who drove Krishna's chariot on the peace embassy?
questions.explanation: Daruka, Krishna's own charioteer, yoked the horses and drove him from Upaplavya to Hastinapura.

questions.q: Which sage in the Kuru court told the cautionary tale of King Dambhodbhava during the peace talks?
questions.explanation: Parashurama recounted how the arrogant Dambhodbhava was humbled by Nara and Narayana, warning Duryodhana against fighting Arjuna and Krishna.

questions.q: What did Krishna do about the gifts and pavilions Dhritarashtra arranged along his route?
options: He sent them to the Pandava camp
He accepted them to honor the king
He declined them until his mission succeeded
questions.explanation: Krishna passed by the lavish arrangements, saying he would accept the Kuru king's honors only after his purpose was achieved.

questions.q: What did Karna beg of Krishna after learning the secret of his birth?
options: To let him duel Arjuna first
To keep the secret, lest Yudhishthira hand him the crown
To protect Duryodhana in the coming war
To tell Kunti that he forgave her
questions.explanation: Karna asked Krishna to conceal his birth, fearing the righteous Yudhishthira would give him the kingdom, which he would only pass to Duryodhana.

questions.q: Parting from Krishna after the failed embassy, Karna described the coming war as what?
questions.explanation: Karna foresaw the war as a vast sacrifice of arms with Krishna as its overseer and the warriors as its offerings.
